title: Dillenia longepetiolata
taxonomy: Dillenia longepetiolata is a species of flowering plant in the family Dilleniaceae. It belongs to the genus Dillenia, which consists of about 40 species of trees and shrubs. The family Dilleniaceae is an ancient lineage of flowering plants that is thought to have originated over 100 million years ago. Dillenia longepetiolata is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in the tropical regions of Malaysia, Indonesia, and the Philippines. The classification of Dillenia longepetiolata reflects its unique evolutionary history and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Dillenia longepetiolata, commonly known as the Long-petioled Dillenia or Dillenia, is a plant species belonging to the family Dilleniaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
